name: Pork and Sauerkraut Goulash
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons Pork Fat or Olive oil
1 cup Sauerkraut (27 oz.) with Caraway seeds
Salt and freshly ground Black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the pork fat or oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Add the cubed pork and brown lightly, turning occasionally until evenly coated on all sides.
2. Remove the pork from the skillet and set aside. In the same skillet, add the chopped onions and green peppers. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are just tender but not soft or mushy.
3. Stir in the Hungarian paprika, bay leaf, water or stock, and tomatoes. Return the pork to the skillet.
4. Cover the skillet and simmer over low heat for 1 hour, stirring once every 15 minutes, or until the meat is very tender and easily pulls away from the bone.
5. Remove the bay leaf. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Serve hot with a dollop of sour cream spooned over the goulash.
